code: spec <- create_spec(   ingest = list(format = "generic"),   schema = list(     raw = list(       datetime = "timestamp_ms",       x = "acc_x",       y = "acc_y",       z = "acc_z",       id = "animal_id",       id_type = "id",       time_format = "unix_ms"     )   ) )

title: Recording Observations
text: Times UTC Unix milliseconds. row describe one labelled bout. id must match animal identifier used training data.

title: Recording Observations
text: table uses different column names ISO timestamps, can map spec:
code: spec <- create_spec(   schema = list(     observations = list(       id = "animal_id",       label = "behaviour",       start = "start_time",       end = "end_time",       time_format = "iso8601_utc"     )   ) )

title: moover
text: moover converts movement data behaviour workflows animal scientists. designed support : beginner-friendly guided workflows reproducible scripted workflows JSON specs designed work locally normal computer, without requiring access high-performance computing (HPC) environment. Larger runs can still take time, goal make model building, optimisation, prediction accessible beginners working machines.

text: ingests raw accelerometer data converts canonical movement format generates epoch features trains optimises Random Forest models evaluates models LOCO metrics plots exports reusable embedded-friendly model bundles predicts behaviour new datasets using existing bundles
